titleStmt.title: Letter from Maurice O'Sullivan to The Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 26 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Maurice O'Sullivan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Maurice O'Sullivan asking if RCPI could change his name from Maurice Hennigan O'Sullivan to Maurice Sullivan Smith on the College's roll. The letter granting approval was initialed by Ephraim MacDowel Cosgrave, RCPI President, and dated 4 February 1916.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Main Street, Bantry, Co, Cork, Ireland

correspAction.persName: The Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Main St.
Bantry.
Co. Cork.
Ireland. 26/1/16. Register.
Royal College of Physicians (Conjoint Board). Dear Sir, I wrote to Mr. Roe. 35 Dawson St.
Dublin, as I want my name on the
Medical Register altered from
Maurice Hennigan O'Sullivan of
P.S. You have my cheque for 6/2 fee.
last time I wrote I would like my name
changed to Maurice Sullivan Fisher.
I am writing to the Registers of the
R.C.S.P.I & sending on £2:2:0 fees.
I am enclosing a cheque for £1:1:0 fee
I am writing by the same post to the
Register of the General Medical Council,
Dublin. also to the Register of the
R. College of Surgeons, Dublin. Hotel Gevalia.
Upper Woburn Place.
London. W. C. 1/3/16. The Register.
R. College of Physicians Dublin. Dear Sir, I would feel grateful if you
would alter my registered name & address
from Maurice Hennigan O'Sullivan of
13 Colebrooke Row, Isington, V. to
Maurice Sullivan, Hotel Gevalia
Upper Woburn Place, London. W.C. I remain, yours faithfully M. Sullivan. P.S. over.
13 Colebrooke Row. Islington. N. to
Maurice Sullivan Smith of 53 Bernard
St. Russell Sq. W.C. I would feel very grateful if you
would be good enough to send him
on a letter saying the the name on the
register has been altered as I desire.
I am writing by the same post to the
Register of the Royal College of Surgeons
also to Mr. Roe. Thanking you at your very earliest
convenience. I remain,
Yours faithfully, MM Sullivan. (1907) Macd.C
4.II.16
